                     ]\n                    };\n                };\n            } else {\n                frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return await this.service.getComments(frame.options);\n    }\n\n    async adminBrowse(frame) {\n        if (frame.options.post_id) {\n            if (frame.options.filter) {\n                frame.options.mongoTransformer = function (query) {\n                    return {\n                        $and: [\n                            {\n                                post_id: frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n            }\n        }\n\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;\n        // Admin routes in Comments-UI lack member context due to cross-domain constraints (CORS), which prevents\n        // credentials from being passed. This causes issues like the inability to determine if a\n        // logged-in admin (acting on behalf of a member) has already liked a comment.\n        // To resolve this, we retrieve the `impersonate_member_uuid` from the request params and\n        // explicitly set it in the context options as the acting member's ID.\n        // Note: This approach is applied to several admin routes where member context is required.\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n        return await this.service.getAdminComments(frame.options);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Browse all comments across the site (admin only, no post_id required)\n     * Used for admin moderation page showing comments from all posts\n     *\n     * Controller responsibility: Parse frame into clean domain parameters.\n     * The frame should not pass beyond this layer.\n     *\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async adminBrowseAll(frame) {\n        // Query params can be strings or booleans depending on how the request is made\n        const includeNestedParam = frame.options.include_nested;\n        const includeNested = includeNestedParam !== 'false' && includeNestedParam !== false;\n\n        // Extract count.reports from filter (it needs special handling as raw SQL)\n        const {filter, reportCount, mongoTransformer} = this.#extractReportCountFilter(frame.options.filter);\n\n        return await this.service.getAdminAllComments({\n            includeNested,\n            filter,\n            mongoTransformer,\n            reportCount,\n            order: frame.options.order || 'created_at desc',\n            page: frame.options.page,\n            limit: frame.options.limit\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async replies(frame) {\n        return this.service.getReplies(frame.options.id, _.omit(frame.options, 'id'));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async adminReplies(frame) {\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;\n        frame.options.order = 'created_at asc'; // we always want to load replies from oldest to newest\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n\n        return this.service.getReplies(frame.options.id, _.omit(frame.options, 'id'));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async read(frame) {\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n        return await this.service.getCommentByID(frame.data.id, frame.options);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async edit(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n\n        let result;\n        if (frame.data.comments[0].status === 'deleted') {\n            result = await this.service.deleteComment(\n                frame.options.id,\n                frame?.options?.context?.member?.id,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        } else {\n            result = await this.service.editCommentContent(\n                frame.options.id,\n                frame?.options?.context?.member?.id,\n                frame.data.comments[0].html,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        }\n\n        if (result) {\n            const postId = result.get('post_id');\n            const parentId = result.get('parent_id');\n            const pathsToInvalidate = [\n                postId ? `/api/members/comments/post/${postId}/` : null,\n                parentId ? `/api/members/comments/${parentId}/replies/` : null\n            ].filter(path => path !== null);\n            frame.setHeader('X-Cache-Invalidate', pathsToInvalidate.join(', '));\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n frame.options\n            );\n        } else {\n            result = await this.service.commentOnPost(\n                data.post_id,\n                frame.options.context.member.id,\n  return result;\n    }\n\n    async destroy() {\n        throw new MethodNotAllowedError({\n            message: tpl(messages.cannotDestroyComments)\n        });\n    }\n\n    async count(frame) {\n        if (!frame?.options?.ids) {\n            return await this.stats.getAllCounts();\n        }\n\n        const ids = frame?.options?.ids.split(',');\n\n        return await tpl = require('@tryghost/tpl');\nconst errors = require('@tryghost/errors');\nconst {MemberCommentEvent} = require('../../../shared/events');\nconst DomainEvents = require('@tryghost/domain-events');\nconst {byNQL} = require('../../models/base/plugins/bulk-filters');\n\nconst messages = {\n    commentNotFound: 'Comment could not be found',\n    memberNotFound: 'Unable to find member',\n    likeNotFound: 'Unable to find like',\n    alreadyLiked: 'This comment was liked already',\n    replyToReply: 'Can not reply to a reply',\n    commentsNotEnabled: 'Comments are not enabled for this site.',\n    cannotCommentOnPost: 'You do not have permission to comment on this post.',\n    cannotEditComment: 'You do not have permission to edit comments'\n};\n\nclass CommentsService {\n    constructor({config, logging, models, mailer, settingsCache, settingsHelpers, urlService, urlUtils, contentGating, labs}) {\n        /** @private */\n        this.models = models;\n\n        /** @private */\n        this.settingsCache = settingsCache;\n\n        /** @private */\n        this.contentGating = contentGating;\n\n        const Emails = require('./comments-service-emails');\n        /** @private */\n        this.emails = new Emails({\n            config,\n            logging,\n            models,\n            mailer,\n            settingsCache,\n            settingsHelpers,\n            urlService,\n            urlUtils,\n            labs\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @returns {'off'|'all'|'paid'}\n     */\n    get enabled() {\n        const setting = this.settingsCache.get('comments_enabled');\n        if (setting === 'off' || setting === 'all' || setting === 'paid') {\n            return setting;\n        }\n        return 'off';\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkEnabled() {\n        if (this.enabled === 'off') {\n            throw new errors.MethodNotAllowedError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentsNotEnabled)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkCommentAccess(memberModel) {\n        if (this.enabled === 'paid' && memberModel.get('status') === 'free') {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel) {\n        const access = this.contentGating.checkPostAccess(postModel.toJSON(), memberModel.toJSON());\n        if (access === this.contentGating.BLOCK_ACCESS) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    async sendNewCommentNotifications(comment) {\n        await this.emails.notifyPostAuthors(comment);\n\n        if (comment.get('parent_id')) {\n            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'parent'});\n        }\n        if (comment.get('in_reply_to_id')) {\n            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'in_reply_to'});\n        }\n    }\n\n    async likeComment(commentId, member, options = {}) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member.id\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const data = {\n            member_id: memberModel.id,\n            comment_id: commentId\n        };\n\n        const existing = await this.models.CommentLike.findOne(data, options);\n\n        if (existing) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.alreadyLiked)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return await this.models.CommentLike.add(data, options);\n    }\n\n    async unlikeComment(commentId, member, options = {}) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n\n        try {\n            await this.models.CommentLike.destroy({\n                ...options,\n                destroyBy: {\n                    member_id: member.id,\n                    comment_id: commentId\n                },\n                require: true\n            });\n        } catch (err) {\n            if (err instanceof this.models.CommentLike.NotFoundError) {\n                return Promise.reject(new errors.NotFoundError({\n                    message: tpl(messages.likeNotFound)\n                }));\n            }\n\n            throw err;\n        }\n    }\n\n    async reportComment(commentId, reporter) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId}, {require: true});\n\n        // Check if this reporter already reported this comment (then don't send an email)?\n        const existing = await this.models.CommentReport.findOne({\n            comment_id: comment.id,\n            member_id: reporter.id\n        });\n\n        if (existing) {\n            // Ignore silently for now\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Save report model\n        await this.models.CommentReport.add({\n            comment_id: comment.id,\n            member_id: reporter.id\n        });\n\n        await this.emails.notifyReport(comment, reporter);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getComments(options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: null});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @typedef {Object} AdminBrowseAllOptions\n     * @property {boolean} includeNested - If true, include replies in flat list; if false, only top-level comments\n     * @property {string[]} [withRelated] - Relations to include (e.g. ['member', 'post'])\n     * @property {string} [filter] - NQL filter string\n     * @property {Function} [mongoTransformer] - Function to transform parsed NQL filter\n     * @property {{op: string, value: number}} [reportCount] - Filter by report count (op: '=', '>', '>=', '<', '<=', '!=')\n     * @property {string} order - Order string (e.g. 'created_at desc')\n     * @property {number} [page] - Page number\n     * @property {number} [limit] - Results per page\n     */\n\n    /**\n     * Browse all comments across the site for admin moderation.\n     * Does not check if comments are enabled - admins can moderate existing comments.\n     *\n     * Service responsibility: Business logic and data access.\n     * Receives clean, typed parameters - no frame/HTTP knowledge.\n     *\n     * @param {AdminBrowseAllOptions} options\n     */\n    async getAdminAllComments({includeNested, filter, mongoTransformer, reportCount, order, page, limit}) {\n        return await this.models.Comment.findPage({\n            withRelated: ['member', 'post', 'count.replies', 'count.likes', 'count.reports', 'inReplyTo', 'parent'],\n            filter,\n            mongoTransformer,\n            reportCount,\n            order,\n            page,\n            limit,\n            parentId: includeNested ? undefined : null,\n            isAdmin: true,\n            browseAll: true\n        });\n    }\n\n    async getAdminComments(options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: null, isAdmin: true});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to get replies from\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getReplies(id, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: id});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get reporters for a comment (admin only)\n     * @param {string} commentId - The ID of the Comment to get reporters for\n     * @param {any} options - Query options (page, limit)\n     */\n    async getCommentReporters(commentId, options = {}) {\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n        const {page, limit} = options;\n        const result = await this.models.CommentReport.findPage({\n            filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n            withRelated: ['member'],\n            order: 'created_at desc',\n            page,\n            limit\n        });\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get likes for a comment (admin only)\n     * @param {string} commentId - The ID of the Comment to get likes for\n     * @param {any} options - Query options (page, limit)\n     */\n    async getCommentLikes(commentId, options = {}) {\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n        const {page, limit} = options;\n        const result = await this.models.CommentLike.findPage({\n            filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n            withRelated: [],\n            order: 'created_at desc',\n            page,\n            limit\n        });\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to get\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getCommentByID(id, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id}, options);\n\n        if (!model) {\n            throw new errors.NotFoundError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} post - The ID of the Post to comment on\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to comment as\n     * @param {string} comment - The HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     * @param {Date} [createdAt] - Optional custom created_at timestamp\n     */\n    async commentOnPost(post, member, comment, options, createdAt) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const postModel = await this.models.Post.findOne({\n            id: post\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['tiers']\n        });\n\n        this.checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel);\n\n        const commentData = {\n            post_id: post,\n            member_id: member,\n            parent_id: null,\n            html: comment,\n            status: 'published'\n        };\n\n        if (createdAt) {\n            commentData.created_at = createdAt;\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.add(commentData, options);\n\n        if (!options.context.internal) {\n            await this.sendNewCommentNotifications(model);\n        }\n\n        DomainEvents.dispatch(MemberCommentEvent.create({\n            memberId: member,\n            postId: post,\n            commentId: model.id\n        }));\n\n        // Instead of returning the model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} parent - The ID of the Comment to reply to\n     * @param {string} inReplyTo - The ID of the Reply to reply to\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to comment as\n     * @param {string} comment - The HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     * @param {Date} [createdAt] - Optional custom created_at timestamp\n     */\n    async replyToComment(parent, inReplyTo, member, comment, options, createdAt) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const parentComment = await this.getCommentByID(parent, options);\n        if (!parentComment) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        if (parentComment.get('parent_id') !== null) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.replyToReply)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const postModel = await this.models.Post.findOne({\n            id: parentComment.get('post_id')\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['tiers']\n        });\n\n        this.checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel);\n\n        let inReplyToComment;\n        if (parent && inReplyTo) {\n            inReplyToComment = await this.getCommentByID(inReplyTo, options);\n\n            // we only allow references to published comments to avoid leaking\n            // hidden data via the snippet included in API responses\n            if (inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('status') !== 'published') {\n                inReplyToComment = null;\n            }\n\n            // we don't allow in_reply_to references across different parents\n            if (inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('parent_id') !== parent) {\n                inReplyToComment = null;\n            }\n        }\n\n        const commentData = {\n            post_id: parentComment.get('post_id'),\n            member_id: member,\n            parent_id: parentComment.id,\n            in_reply_to_id: inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('id'),\n            html: comment,\n            status: 'published'\n        };\n\n        if (createdAt) {\n            commentData.created_at = createdAt;\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.add(commentData, options);\n\n        if (!options.context.internal) {\n            await this.sendNewCommentNotifications(model);\n        }\n\n        DomainEvents.dispatch(MemberCommentEvent.create({\n            memberId: member,\n            postId: parentComment.get('post_id'),\n            commentId: model.id\n        }));\n\n        // Instead of returning the model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to delete\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to delete as\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async deleteComment(id, member, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const existingComment = await this.getCommentByID(id, options);\n\n        if (existingComment.get('member_id') !== member) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                // todo fix message\n                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.edit({\n            status: 'deleted'\n        }, {\n            id,\n            require: true,\n            ...options\n        });\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to edit\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to edit as\n     * @param {string} comment - The new HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async editCommentContent(id, member, comment, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const existingComment = await this.getCommentByID(id, options);\n\n        if (!comment) {\n            return existingComment;\n        }\n\n        if (existingComment.get('member_id') !== member) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotEditComment)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.edit({\n            html: comment,\n            edited_at: new Date()\n        }, {\n            id,\n            require: true,\n            ...options\n        });\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Bulk update comment status based on NQL filter\n     * @param {string} filter - NQL filter string (e.g., \"member_id:'abc123'+status:published\")\n     * @param {string} status - New status ('hidden', 'published', 'deleted')\n     */\n    async bulkUpdateStatus(filter, status) {\n        await this.models.Comment.bulkEditWhere({\n            data: {status},\n            where: byNQL(filter)\n        });\n    }\n\n    async getMemberIdByUUID(uuid, options) {\n        const member = await this.models.Member.findOne({uuid}, options);\n\n        if (!member) {\n            throw new errors.NotFoundError({\n                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return member.id;\n    }\n}\n\nmodule.exports = CommentsService;\n","numLines":522,"startLine":1,"totalLines":522}}}

But I saw in the controller that there is a `getCommentReporters` method that uses `frame.options.id`.\n\nLet me verify the issue one more time by looking at what the getCommentReporters does vs what getCommentLikes does.\n\nFrom the comments-controller.js file I read:\n- Line 357-363: `getCommentReporters` uses `frame.options.id`\n- Line 369-375: `getCommentLikes` uses `frame.data.id`\n\nAnd in the comment-likes.js endpoint:\n```javascript\noptions: [\n    'id',\n    'page',\n    'limit'\n],\n```\n\nThe `id` is in options, not in the body. So it should be `frame.options.id`, not `frame.data.id`.\n\nThis is definitely a bug.\n\nNow let me finalize my analysis:\n\n### Defect 1: Incorrect frame property access\n- **File**: comments-controller.js\n- **Line**: 370\n- **Severity**: critical\n- **Description**: The `getCommentLikes` method uses `frame.data.id` to get the comment ID, but the endpoint definition specifies `id` in the `options` array (query parameters), not in the request body. This should be `frame.options.id`. This is inconsistent with the similar `getCommentReporters` method which correctly uses `frame.options.id`. Result: The comment ID will be undefined, causing the request to fail.\n- **Fix**: Change `frame.data.id` to `frame.options.id`\n\n### Defect 2: Missing comment existence verification\n- **File**: comments-service.js\n- **Line**: 261\n- **Severity**: high  \n- **Description**: The `getCommentLikes` method queries for a comment but doesn't use the result and doesn't pass `{require: true}` to verify it exists. Medium Severity: Compliance Violation**\n**File**: `comment-likes.js` (line 8)\n- **Issue**: Missing `headers` configuration in the browse endpoint\n- **Impact**: Violates Ghost's API conventions.
